reasoning: The Roberts Court has consistently ruled against campaign finance limitations on First Amendment grounds (e.g., Citizens United, McCutcheon, Cruz). In this case, the Trump DOJ and FEC explicitly declined to defend the constitutionality of the coordinated spending limits, forcing the DNC to intervene. During the December 2025 oral arguments, conservative justices seemed receptive to the NRSC's position; Justice Kavanaugh explicitly expressed concern that the current limits harm political parties relative to outside groups (which strongly aligns with the plaintiffs' core argument), and Justice Barrett pressed the DNC lawyer on the necessity of the limits. Given the 6-3 conservative supermajority, the historical base rate of the Roberts Court striking down similar restrictions, the administration's active non-defense of the law, and favorable signals during oral arguments, it is highly probable that the Court will issue a merits decision by summer 2026 that strikes down or significantly weakens these limits.
